title: Edema and Proteinuria - Nephrotic Syndrome
chief_complaint: Progressive swelling of legs and around eyes for 2 weeks, foamy urine, weight gain of 6 kg.

physical_exam.general: Periorbital edema, bilateral pitting edema to knees

hidden_data.renal_biopsy: Membranous nephropathy (thickened GBM, spike and dome on silver stain)

key_learning_points: Membranous = most common cause in adults

patient_responses.swelling: My shoes don't fit, my legs are huge
patient_responses.urine: It's really foamy, like beer foam
patient_responses.weight: I gained 6 kg in 2 weeks without eating more

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Nephrotic syndrome from membranous nephropathy.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is nephrotic syndrome. The >3.5g proteinuria + hypoalbuminemia confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Membranous nephropathy: ACEi/ARB for proteinuria, statins for hyperlipidemia, diuretics for edema. Check anti-PLA2R. Immunosuppression if progressive. Monitor for spontaneous remission (30%).
